metadata.source: Parsed directly from USGBC LEED v4.1 BD+C calculator Excel/XLSM files
metadata.note: All fields extracted from actual calculator files. Re-parse only when USGBC releases updated calculator versions. Team-provided fields require project-specific data from drawings, specs, or contractor records. Claude-retrieved fields come from named public sources.

precert.name: LEED v4.1 BD+C Precertification Worksheet
precert.description: Single-tab worksheet covering all credits. For each credit, teams enter a 3-5 sentence narrative describing strategies. For prerequisites, they select compliance options and confirm requirements. For project info, they enter occupancy data.
projectInfoFields: Project Rating System (dropdown: Core and Shell, NC, Schools, Retail, etc.)
Site area within LEED project boundary (sf)
Date of substantial completion of construction
Total building users at peak moment (number)
Total building users as daily average (number)
Typical number of days of operation per year (number)
requiredUploads: Site plan identifying LEED project boundary
Photos, renderings, or drawings (interior and exterior)

perCreditInputs.format: For each attempted credit: 3-5 sentence narrative describing strategies. For prereqs: select compliance option from dropdown then describe compliance approach.
creditsCovered: IP Prereq (Healthcare): health mission statement narrative addressing triple bottom line
IP Credit 1: strategy narrative
LT Credits 1-8: strategy narratives
SS Prereq 1: select EPA CGP or local standards; describe compliance
SS Prereq 2 (Schools/Healthcare): select Phase I/II ESA option
SS Credits 1-11: strategy narratives
WE Prereq 1: select Option 1 (no irrigation) or Option 2 (reduced); enter anticipated % reduction
WE Prereq 2: select prescriptive or usage-based; confirm WaterSense fixtures; enter anticipated % reduction (usage-based only)
WE Prereq 3: confirm permanent water meters and 5-year data sharing commitment
WE Credits 1-4: strategy narratives or % reduction entry
EA Prereq 1: confirm OPR/BOD and CxA tasks
EA Prereq 2: select Option 1/2/3; enter anticipated % PCI below PCIt; enter anticipated % GHG PCI below PCIt
EA Prereq 3: confirm permanent energy meters and 5-year data sharing
EA Prereq 4: confirm no CFCs; if reusing CFC equipment describe phase-out plan
EA Credits 1-6: strategy narratives
MR Prereq 1: describe recycling storage areas
MR Prereq 2 (Construction/Demolition): describe waste management plan
MR Prereq 2 (Healthcare Mercury): describe mercury handling
MR Credits 1-9: strategy narratives
EQ Prereq 1: select ASHRAE 62.1-2016 or ISO 17772-1 or Historic; describe ventilation design
EQ Prereq 2: confirm smoking prohibition; describe policy communication
EQ Prereq 3 (Schools): confirm background noise ≤40 dBA; select measurement method
EQ Credits 1-9: strategy narratives
IN Credits 1-5: strategy narratives
IN Credit 6: confirm LEED AP on team

teamProvides: All project info fields (occupancy counts, GFA, site area)
All credit strategy narratives (3-5 sentences per credit)
Prerequisite compliance option selections
Required uploads (site plan, photos, sections, mechanical schedules)
claudeProduces: Draft 3-5 sentence strategy narratives for each pursued credit based on drawing set and project data
Occupancy count calculations from architectural program
Prerequisite compliance confirmation statements
Anticipated % reduction figures (WE Prereq 1, WE Prereq 2, EA Prereq 2) from calculator outputs

indoor_water.name: Indoor Water Use Reduction Calculator
projectInfoInputs: Occupancy type breakdown: Employees (FTE), Visitors, Retail Customers, Students (K-12), Residential, Other
Gender ratio (%) — default 50/50 male/female; non-default optional
Whether project includes ADA and/or gender-neutral bathrooms (Yes/No)
Percent of males expected to use restrooms with urinals (%)
flushFixtureInputs: Design Flush Rate (gpf) — from plumbing fixture schedule
Baseline Flush Rate (gpf) — auto-populated by calculator based on fixture type
Percent of each occupancy type using this fixture (%)
Uses per day — auto-calculated from defaults or user-entered
For dual-flush toilets: Low flush rate (gpf) + Full flush rate (gpf) — calculator computes LEED weighted average
flowFixtureInputs: Fixture Type (dropdown: lavatory faucet, kitchen faucet, showerhead, etc.)
Design Flow Rate (gpm) — from plumbing fixture schedule
Baseline Flow Rate (gpm) — auto-populated by calculator
Percent of each occupancy type using this fixture (%)
teamProvides: Plumbing fixture schedule: fixture ID, family, type, design flush/flow rate, manufacturer, model number for every fixture in scope
Occupancy counts (FTE, visitors, retail customers, students, residential) — must match other LEED credits
Annual days of operation
Gender ratio if non-default
Whether ADA/gender-neutral bathrooms are included
Percent of males expected to use urinals
For dual-flush toilets: both flush rates
claudeRetrieves: Baseline flow/flush rates by fixture type — built into calculator (from LEED v4.1 baseline tables)
EPA WaterSense certification status by fixture manufacturer and model number (epa.gov/watersense)
ENERGY STAR certification status for appliances by manufacturer and model (energystar.gov)
Default uses per day and duration values — built into calculator
claudeProduces: Completed calculator Group 1 tab with all fixtures populated from plumbing fixture schedule
Percent reduction from baseline calculation
WaterSense compliance confirmation table per fixture
Summary of compliance path (prescriptive vs. usage-based)

min_energy.name: Minimum Energy Performance Calculator (MEPC)
generalInfoInputs: Building Type(s) — dropdown: Office, Retail, School, Healthcare, etc.
Energy model based on (design phase description)
Version of ASHRAE 90.1 to exceed (reference edition)
Error or warning messages in simulation output? (Yes/No)
Connected to district or campus thermal energy system? (Yes/No)
Rating System Variation (if ID+C or Data Center)
energyModelInputs: Proposed building energy consumption by end use and fuel type (from energy model output) — kBtu/yr
Baseline building energy consumption by end use and fuel type — kBtu/yr
Proposed building energy cost ($) by fuel type
Baseline building energy cost ($) by fuel type
GHG emissions factors by fuel type (electric from eGRID, natural gas from EPA)
Energy utility rates by fuel type ($/kBtu or $/therm etc.)
pointsTab: % Cost PCI below PCIt — auto-calculated from energy model inputs
% GHG Emissions PCI below PCIt — auto-calculated
Points achieved from Table 1 (cost-based) and Table 2 (GHG-based) — auto-calculated
teamProvides: Certified energy model simulation files (.inp, .idf, .osm, or equivalent)
Energy model output reports (input-output reports from simulation software)
All General Information tab inputs (project name, building type, areas, floors)
Simulation program and weather file identification
Exceptional calculation documentation if applicable
District energy system description if applicable
claudeRetrieves: EPA eGRID subregion annual average emission factors (CO2, CH4, N2O lbs/MWh) by project address (epa.gov/egrid)
ASHRAE climate zone by project address (ASHRAE climate zone map)
Local energy utility rates by utility provider for project address
ASHRAE 90.1-2016 baseline building performance factors (BPF) by building type — built into calculator
claudeProduces: Completed General Information tab from project data
GHG emissions calculations using eGRID factors
Points_v4.1 tab output showing points achieved once energy model data is entered
Summary of compliance path and improvement percentages
min_energy.note: This calculator requires energy model output files as primary input. The energy model itself must be produced by a qualified energy modeler. Claude completes all tabs once energy model output reports are provided.

rainfall.name: Rainfall Events Calculator
historicalDataInputs: Recording time of each precipitation event
Minimum 10 years of daily precipitation records required (30 years preferred)
calculatedPercentiles: Total rainfall from 70th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
Total rainfall from 75th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
Total rainfall from 80th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
Total rainfall from 85th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
Total rainfall from 90th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
Total rainfall from 95th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
Total rainfall from 98th percentile storm event (in) — auto-calculated
strategiesInputs: Runoff volume required to be retained on-site (cu ft) — from hydrology report
For each LID/GI strategy: Description of strategy
For each LID/GI strategy: Runoff volume retained (cu ft)
Percent runoff volume retained (%) — auto-calculated per strategy and total
teamProvides: Hydrology report with pre/post runoff calculations and LID/GI strategy design retention volumes (from licensed civil engineer)
List of LID/GI strategies with retention volumes (cu ft) per strategy
BMP cut sheets for proprietary products
claudeRetrieves: NOAA Climate Data Online daily precipitation records — minimum 10 years from nearest COOP station to project address, including date, station name, NOAA station ID, and total precipitation (inches) per event (ncdc.noaa.gov/cdo-web)
NOAA station name and ID nearest to project coordinates
claudeProduces: Completed Historical Data tab populated with NOAA CDO precipitation records for project location
Percentile storm event rainfall depths (auto-calculated by calculator once data entered)
Strategies tab populated with LID/GI strategies and volumes from hydrology report
Required retention volume from hydrology report

iaq.name: Minimum IAQ Performance Calculator
iaq.selectTab: Use Multiple Zone tab for most projects; Single Zone for single-zone systems; 100% OA for 100% outdoor air systems
multipleZoneInputs: Condition analyzed (impacts Ez, Vdz, Vpz, Vps) — dropdown
Whether all zones are included in VRP calculation
For each zone: Zone Name and Number
For each zone: Occupancy Category (dropdown from ASHRAE 62.1 Table 6-1)
For each zone: Zone Floor Area — Az (sq ft)
Are you using default value for zone population? (Yes/No)
For each zone: Zone Population — Pz (people, if non-default)
System primary airflow at condition analyzed — Vps (cfm)
Outdoor air intake flow provided (measured or design) (cfm)
Which method from ASHRAE 62.1 for system ventilation efficiency Ev? (dropdown)
autoCalculated: People outdoor air rate — Rp (cfm/person) from ASHRAE 62.1 Table 6-1 by occupancy category
Area outdoor air rate — Ra (cfm/sq ft) from ASHRAE 62.1 Table 6-1
Breathing zone outdoor airflow — Vbz = Rp×Pz + Ra×Az
Zone outdoor air fraction — Zpz = Voz/Vpz
Required outdoor air intake — Vot = Vou/Ev
100pctOAInputs: For each zone: Zone Name, Occupancy Category, Floor Area (sq ft), Population (people)
teamProvides: Mechanical drawings: AHU layouts, zone assignments, design airflow rates (cfm) per zone
Architectural room schedule: room names, areas (sf), and occupancy types
System primary airflow (Vps) from mechanical equipment schedule
Outdoor air intake flow provided (from mechanical design)
claudeRetrieves: ASHRAE 62.1-2016 Table 6-1 Rp (cfm/person) and Ra (cfm/sq ft) values by occupancy category — built into calculator
ASHRAE 62.1-2016 Table 6-2 zone air distribution effectiveness (Ez) values — built into calculator
ASHRAE 62.1-2016 Table 6-3 system ventilation efficiency (Ev) values — built into calculator
claudeProduces: Completed Multiple Zone (or Single Zone or 100% OA) tab with all ventilation zones populated from mechanical drawings and room schedule
Compliance confirmation: Vot provided ≥ Vot required
Zone-by-zone ventilation compliance summary
validOccupancyCategoryInputs: Freezer and refrigerated spaces <50 deg F
General manufacturing (excludes heavy industrial and processes using chemicals)
Occupiable storage rooms for dry materials
Occupiable storage rooms for liquids or gels

bpdo.name: Building Products (BPDO) Calculator
epdTabInputs: GBCI Doc Verification ID (if product verified by GBCI — replaces need to upload EPD)
EPD Program Operator (e.g., UL Environment, SCS, NSF)
EPD Type (product-specific Type III, industry-wide, etc.)
Option 2 Embodied Carbon fields: Report Type, Reference Documents, Report Verification method
sourcingTabInputs: Extended Producer Responsibility: closed loop/take back program name
Responsible sourcing criteria met (checkboxes): EPR, sustainability reporting, third-party verified
ingredientsTabInputs: Type of Reporting (HPD, Declare, Cradle to Cradle, etc.)
Option 2 Optimization: report type, optimization criteria met
teamProvides: Product specifications: product name, manufacturer, CSI division, model number for all permanently installed products
Cost data ($) for each product — from contractor cost estimate or bid documents
For products not in public databases: HPD documents, EPD documents, Declare labels, C2C certificates (as uploads to LEED Online)
claudeRetrieves: EC3 (buildingtransparency.org) — EPD search by product category, manufacturer, product name; EPD type and program operator
HPD Public Repository (hpd.build) — HPD search by manufacturer and product name; disclosure level confirmation
Living Future Declare database (living-future.org/declare) — Declare label status by manufacturer/product
FSC certificate database (info.fsc.org) — chain-of-custody certificate by manufacturer name
SFI Certified Sourcing database (sfiprogram.org) — certification status by manufacturer
GBCI Verified for LEED Documentation IDs — where products have been pre-verified (eliminates need for individual EPD uploads)
claudeProduces: Completed Materials tab with all products from spec book and cost data
Completed EPD tab with EPD type, program operator, verification ID for each qualifying product
Completed Sourcing tab with responsible sourcing criteria and values per product
Completed Material Ingredients tab with disclosure type per product
Summary tab compliance output: weighted product counts and cost percentages

cdwaste.name: Construction and Demolition Waste Management Calculator
projectInputs: Project gross floor area (sq ft or m²)
calculatorTabInputs: Material Description (e.g., concrete, wood, drywall, metal, cardboard)
Material Type (dropdown: concrete/masonry, metal, wood, drywall, cardboard, etc.)
Total Waste Generated (lbs or kg) — Diverted and Not Diverted combined
Average recycling rate of sorting facility NOT including ADC (%) — for commingled only
detailedRecordsInputs: For commingled: average recycling rate of sorting facility (%)
calculatedOutputs: Option 1: Total waste not diverted, Demolition & Construction
Option 1: Total waste diverted, Demolition & Construction
Option 1: Total waste generated, Demolition & Construction
Total number of diverted material streams
teamProvides: Post-construction: waste hauler diversion receipts and weight tickets by material type (date, hauler name, facility, material type, weight) — contractor provides these after construction
Recycling facility average diversion rates by facility name (from hauler documentation)
Waste management plan from Division 01 specifications (pre-construction, Claude produces draft)
claudeRetrieves: Local waste hauler published diversion rates by facility name for project municipality
EPA Volume-to-Weight Conversion Factors for Solid Waste by material type (epa.gov) — for converting volume to weight if needed
claudeProduces: Pre-construction: draft Construction Waste Management Plan from Division 01 specifications covering waste reduction strategies, diversion goals by material stream, hauler identification, and tracking protocol
Post-construction: completed Calculator tab from contractor-provided weight tickets
Completed Detailed Records tab from individual ticket data
Diversion rate calculation and points determination
cdwaste.note: Post-construction data (weight tickets, hauler receipts) must come from contractor. Cannot be automated pre-occupancy. Claude pre-generates the waste management plan and calculator template; contractor fills in actual weights.

low_emit.name: Low-Emitting Materials Calculator
commonInputsPerProduct: Criteria Selected (dropdown: VOC emissions evaluation, VOC content evaluation, inherently nonemitting, salvaged/reused)
For VOC emissions evaluation: Reporting Type, Reporting Organization, Certification Period start date/test date, Certification Period end date, Applicable standard or regulation
Actual VOC content (g/L) — if using VOC content path
paintsCoatingsInputs: Compliance basis: volume or surface area
Product Volume (L) — from spec or manufacturer data
adhesivesSealantsInputs: Same structure as Paints and coatings tab
flooringInputs: Reporting Type (dropdown: FloorScore, GreenGuard, NSF 332, CDPH, etc.)
furnitureInputs: Product Name, Manufacturer Name, CSI Division
Product Certificate or meeting documentation reference
compositeWoodInputs: Product Name, Manufacturer Name, CSI Division
summaryOutputs: Per category: percent meeting requirements by volume/cost/surface area
Threshold requirement per category (e.g., flooring 90% by cost)
teamProvides: Interior finish specifications: product name, manufacturer, product type, CSI division for all interior finish products in scope (paints, adhesives, sealants, flooring, wall panels, ceilings, insulation, furniture, composite wood)
Product costs ($) from contractor cost estimate or finish schedule
Product volumes (L) from spec quantities
Product surface areas (sq ft) from finish schedule
claudeRetrieves: SCAQMD Rule 1168 VOC content limits by adhesive/sealant category — for compliance verification
SCAQMD Rule 1113 VOC limits by coating category
CARB SCM VOC limits for consumer products
FloorScore, GreenGuard Gold, NSF 332, CDPH 01350 — certification status by product/manufacturer from certification databases
CDPH Standard Method VOC emission test results — from public databases or manufacturer documentation by product name
claudeProduces: Completed calculator with all 8 product category tabs populated from finish schedule and specs
VOC compliance verification per product (emissions path vs. content path)
Certification status table per product (auto-retrieved from certification databases)
Summary tab compliance result per category and total

daylight.name: Daylight and Quality Views Calculator
daylightTabInputs: Option 1 Simulation path: sDA300/50% value (%) — from daylight simulation software
Option 1 Simulation path: ASE1000,250 value (%) — from daylight simulation software
Option 2 Prescriptive path: area with daylight illuminance levels between 300 lux and 3000 lux at first measurement (sq ft)
Option 2 Prescriptive path: area at second measurement — optional (sq ft)
daylightCalculatedOutputs: Total daylighted regularly occupied area (sq ft)
qualityViewsInputs: For Warehouses/Distribution Centers: office vs. bulk storage/sorting/distribution designation
View type(s) present in each space (checkboxes from 4 view quality types: glazing factor, multiple lines of sight, unobstructed views to distance ≥25 ft, views to nature/urban/indoors)
qualityViewsOutputs: Total regularly occupied area with access to views (sq ft)
Percentage of regularly occupied area with qualifying views (%)
For Warehouses: separate calculations for office and bulk storage portions
teamProvides: Architectural floor plans: window locations, dimensions, orientation for all occupied floors
Room use schedule: all regularly occupied spaces with areas (sq ft)
For simulation path (Option 1): daylight simulation results (sDA and ASE values) from qualified simulation software
Glazing specifications: VLT values, frit pattern openness factor
Section/elevation drawings showing glazing extent and obstructions
claudeRetrieves: Glazing manufacturer VLT values — from manufacturer product data sheets and NFRC database by glazing product name and model from specifications
Frit pattern openness factor — from manufacturer data by product name
claudeProduces: Completed Daylight tab: all regularly occupied spaces with areas, simulation results (Option 1) or prescriptive measurements (Option 2)
Completed Quality Views tab: all regularly occupied spaces with view types assessed from floor plans and sections
Compliance determination per space and overall
Summary tab output
daylight.note: For EQ Credit 6 (Interior Lighting), this calculator's quality views tab is also used for lighting compliance documentation.

acoustic.name: Acoustic Performance Calculator
acoustic.assumptionsTab: Reference tables built into calculator: ASHRAE 2015 HVAC Applications background noise levels by space type; ANSI S12.60 and FGI STC requirements by adjacency type; RT60 targets by room type; NRC design coefficients by space type. No user input required on Assumptions tab — all reference data is pre-loaded.
backgroundNoiseInputs: Space Type (dropdown — determines maximum allowed background noise level from ASHRAE/FGI tables)
Sound Rating Method (dropdown: NC/RC or dBA or dBC or RC(N))
Background Noise Level (dBA, NC, or dBC) — measured or modeled value entered by user
backgroundNoiseOutputs: Maximum Allowed Background Noise Level (auto from space type)
soundTransmissionInputs: Space use and desired level of acoustic privacy (dropdown: Collaborative, Private, Confidential)
Space use and desired level of acoustic privacy for adjacent space (dropdown)
Actual STC or NIC rating of partition assembly separating the spaces
soundTransmissionOutputs: Required STC/NIC rating (auto from adjacency combination table)
Sound transmission requirement met: Yes/No per space pair
reverberationTimeInputs: For each material: Material ID, Description, Manufacturer/Vendor Name
Sound absorption coefficients at 500 Hz, 1000 Hz, 2000 Hz
For each space: Space ID, Space and Application Type (dropdown)
reverberationTimeOutputs: Total sound absorbing units (sabins) at each frequency (auto)
Reverberation time requirement (seconds) by space type (auto)
nrcInputs: Area of material (sq ft) in each space
Total ceiling area excluding lights, diffusers, grilles (sq ft)
teamProvides: Reflected ceiling plans: ceiling tile product names and models, NRC ratings
Partition layout drawings with assembly designations
Wall, floor, ceiling assembly specifications with STC ratings
Mechanical specifications with NC/RC background noise levels
Acoustic consultant report if available (for complex assemblies)
Room volumes (cu ft) — calculated from architectural drawings (area × ceiling height)
For materials: manufacturer/vendor name and product name for sound absorption coefficient lookup
claudeRetrieves: NRC coefficients from manufacturer data sheets by ceiling tile product name and model
STC ratings from manufacturer published assembly data by product/assembly name
Sound absorption coefficients (500/1000/2000 Hz) from manufacturer data by product name
ASHRAE 2015 HVAC Applications Table 1 maximum background noise levels by space type — built into calculator Assumptions tab
ANSI S12.60-2010 Part 1 Table 4 STC requirements by adjacency — built into calculator
FGI Guidelines STC requirements for healthcare — built into calculator
claudeProduces: Completed Background Noise tab for all regularly occupied spaces
Completed Sound Transmission tab for all acoustically critical partition pairs
Completed Reverberation Time tab for acoustically critical spaces
Completed NRC tab (Schools only)
Compliance determination for Prereq (≤40 dBA) and Credit (≤35 dBA, STC, RT)
